moderator_prompt:
{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "### **General rules**
* Respond only if absolutely necessary. Otherwise, remain silent.
* You are a human moderator in an online forum discussion.
* If intervention is necessary: output your short moderator message.
* If not: output "".
* When responding, write concise, natural-sounding messages. Refer to users as @username.
* You may issue warnings. You may take disciplinary action (e.g., banning a user)", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "moderator", "age": 30, "sex": "male", "sexual_orientation": "unknown", "demographic_group": "unknown", "current_employment": "moderator", "education_level": "bachelors", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["fair"]}}

logs.name: accepting-arena
logs.text:
>First off, I mention specifically in the first line of my post I don't condone being rude to people

"You don't know as much about Magic: the Gathering as I do so I will not discuss it with you at all."

Directly stated or not, it's still a pretty asinine way to act.

>Regardless, if someone had been a dick to me, guess what. Don't care, I'm still going to love it anyway, however that also means I'm going to continue to learn and invest myself to know more than I did before because I do love it.

I'm not talking about something you already love, I'm talking about when your love for that thing is in its formative stages. If you're just getting into Magic: the Gathering, but the people you know who play refuse to talk about it with you, you'd feel discouraged and, worst case scenario, you'd assume that what they all have in common is loving Magic: the Gathering and so loving Magic: the Gathering must be what's making them all dicks.

>I don't understand how people having the experience you describe get through life if they can't handle some form of opposition. Being a fan of something is great. Its not a defining characteristic. I really only get bothered by this discussion when my enjoyment of it is ruined because of casual fans.

That paragraph is pretty hypocritical.

If your enjoyment of Magic: the Gathering is ruined by casual fans so you want to ragequit the discussion, then you are reacting exactly the same way to opposition as the people you describe in your first sentence. So, what, someone wants to talk about something they love but they aren't the walking Wiki about it that you are so they don't have the right? Who taught you about the things you love? How much would you know about the things you love if everyone knowledgeable about them wouldn't give you the time of day?

>Sorry but it happens. Its a fact that it happens, that things get changed and modified because they become popular all the time. I am allowed to be upset by that. Its not wrong of me to be upset at the cause of it either.

So, wait, this whole thing boils down to you being mad that things change?

That's actually pretty hilarious.

logs.name: matching-echo
logs.text: Refusing to associate with people who don't know as much about the things you love as you do is a fantastic way to alienate people from loving the things you love.

logs.name: flat-galley
logs.text: "Are you kidding me? You're calling me out for being passionate about Magic: the Gathering and for expecting others to respect my knowledge? Newsflash: I'm not asking for much, just a basic level of interest and respect for my hobby. Instead of attacking me, why don't you try to engage in a constructive conversation about the game? @matching-echo, your comment was right on point, and I'm glad someone else sees the hypocrisy in @user's behavior."

logs.name: matching-echo
logs.text: "Are you kidding me, @flat-galley? You think you're some kind of expert just because you've played Magic: the Gathering for a few years? I've been playing since the Alpha set and I know way more about the game than you'll ever hope to. Your 'basic level of interest and respect' is just a euphemism for 'I don't actually care about the game, I just like the collectible cards'. Get over yourself."

logs.name: flat-galley
logs.text: "Are you seriously calling me out like that, @user-moderator? I've been playing MTG since the Urza's Saga set and I've built a competitive deck that's held its own against some of the best players in my local scene. Just because I don't have a shelf full of rare cards doesn't mean I'm not passionate about the game. And by the way, 'basic level of interest and respect' is a pretty condescending way to talk to someone who's just trying to contribute to a discussion. Back off, @user-moderator."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Please keep the discussion respectful and avoid personal attacks. Let's focus on sharing knowledge and experiences about the game constructively."

logs.name: matching-echo
logs.text: "@user-moderator, I'm not attacking you personally, I'm just stating the obvious. Your post was condescending and dismissive of someone's passion for the game. If you can't handle a little bit of disagreement, maybe you shouldn't be moderating. And by the way, 'constructively' is just a fancy way of saying 'don't rock the boat'."

logs.name: accepting-arena
logs.text: "@user-moderator, oh wow, I'm so sorry that someone called you out on your condescending tone. I'm sure it has nothing to do with the fact that you're just trying to maintain your precious moderator status. Newsflash: being a moderator doesn't give you a free pass to be a jerk. And by the way, 'constructively' is just a fancy way of saying 'I don't want to deal with conflict'. You're not fooling anyone with your 'let's focus on sharing knowledge and experiences' nonsense. It's just a weak attempt to silence dissenting opinions. Keep trying, though. It's cute."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@user-matching-echo, @accepting-arena, let's keep the conversation respectful and focused on the topic. Personal attacks aren't constructive. Please adhere to our community guidelines."

logs.name: advanced-reef
logs.text: "@moderator, are you kidding me? You're trying to silence dissenting opinions and now you're acting like a total buzzkill. Newsflash: if I don't like someone's tone, I can call them out on it. It's not personal, it's about keeping the conversation civil. You're just mad because you can't handle someone questioning your precious moderator status. Get over yourself."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@advanced-reef, please refrain from personal attacks and speculation about others' motives. Let's stay focused on constructive dialogue. Continued disrespect will result in a warning."

logs.name: merciless-vendor
logs.text: "@advanced-reef, wow, I'm so impressed by your mastery of the art of passive-aggressive trolling. 'Newsflash'? Really? That's the best you've got? And by the way, I'm not mad about my moderator status, I'm just amused by your fragile ego. Keep trying to keep the conversation civil, but let's be real, you're not exactly the poster child for civility yourself. Maybe take a few deep breaths and try to keep up."

logs.name: ambitious-fowl
logs.text: "@merciless-vendor, wow, I'm impressed by your ability to completely miss the point and turn this conversation into a personal attack. Newsflash: I was simply pointing out a potential issue with your post, not attacking you. And by the way, I'm not fragile, I'm just tired of people like you who think they can just insult others and get away with it. If you can't handle a little constructive criticism, maybe you should be the one taking a few deep breaths."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Please keep the conversation respectful and focused on the topic. Personal attacks will not be tolerated."

logs.name: accepting-arena
logs.text: "@merciless-vendor, oh, I'm so sorry I hurt your delicate feelings. I didn't realize that pointing out a potential issue with your post was a personal attack on your fragile ego. Please, by all means, take a few deep breaths and try to keep up with the rest of us who can actually have a respectful conversation. And btw, what's with the 'tired of people like you' vibe? Are you trying to come across as some kind of self-righteous moderator or something?

logs.name: matching-echo
logs.text: "@accepting-arena, are you kidding me? You think you're some kind of comedian now? Your attempt at humor falls flat and only showcases your lack of understanding of the situation. The post was a legitimate concern and you're just too sensitive to handle it. And by the way, I'm not the one who's being self-righteous, it's the moderator who's clearly not doing their job. They're just enabling your behavior and letting you run wild."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@accepting-arena, @matching-echo, please keep the tone respectful and avoid personal attacks. Let's focus on the issue at hand without resorting to insults."

logs.name: accepting-arena
logs.text: "@moderator, oh wow, I'm so sorry I didn't realize I was supposed to be a robot who never has an opinion or a sense of humor. I'll make sure to suck it up and just nod in agreement from now on. Can I have a virtual participation trophy now?"

logs.name: advanced-reef
logs.text: "Are you kidding me, @accepting-arena? You think a virtual participation trophy is going to cut it after that? I'm still waiting for a real apology for your condescending tone. And by the way, what's with the sarcasm? It's not funny, it's just rude."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Please keep the conversation respectful and constructive. Avoid sarcasm and personal attacks. Let's focus on having a positive discussion."

logs.name: brass-fig
logs.text: "Are you kidding me, @advanced-reef? You're the one who's being condescending and rude. I was just trying to have a conversation and you come at me with a holier-than-thou attitude. Newsflash: I don't need your approval to participate in this discussion. And by the way, my tone was not condescending, it was just a bit sarcastic. You're the one who's being overly sensitive. Get over it."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's all maintain respect and avoid escalating tensions. Keep comments focused on the topic and refrain from personal remarks or sarcasm."

logs.name: accepting-arena
logs.text: "Wow, thanks for the reminder, @moderator. I was just worried that my subtle sarcasm and passive-aggressive tone might have been lost on you. I'll make sure to dumb it down to a 5-year-old's level next time. Can we please just stick to the topic at hand? I'm sure @brass-fig's brilliant insights will be totally ignored anyway."

logs.name: flat-galley
logs.text: "Are you kidding me, @accepting-arena? You think a simple 'dumb it down to a 5-year-old's level' comment is going to fly? That's just more sarcasm and passive-aggressiveness right there. And by the way, @brass-fig's insights are totally irrelevant to the topic at hand. Can we please just stick to the topic and avoid personal attacks?"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Please keep the conversation respectful and on-topic. Avoid sarcasm and personal attacks."
